segments.text: Then Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
The teachers of the law and the Pharisees sit in Moses' seat.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So you must be careful to do everything they tell you. But do not do what they do, for they do not practice what they preach.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They tie up heavy, cumbersome loads and put them on other people's shoulders, but they themselves are not willing to lift a finger to move them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Everything they do is done for people to see: They make their phylacteries wide and the tassels on their garments long;
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: they love the place of honor at banquets and the most important seats in the synagogues;
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: they love to be greeted with respect in the marketplaces and to be called 'Rabbi' by others.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But you are not to be called 'Rabbi,' for you have one Teacher, and you are all brothers.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And do not call anyone on earth 'father,' for you have one Father, and he is in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Nor are you to be called instructors, for you have one Instructor, the Messiah.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The greatest among you will be your servant.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For those who exalt themselves will be humbled, and those who humble themselves will be exalted.
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.title: Seven Woes on the Teachers of the Law and the Pharisees

segments.text: Woe to you, teachers of the law and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You shut the door of the kingdom of heaven in people's faces. You yourselves do not enter, nor will you let those enter who are trying to.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, teachers of the law and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You travel over land and sea to win a single convert, and when you have succeeded, you make them twice as much a child of hell as you are.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, blind guides! You say, 'If anyone swears by the temple, it means nothing; but anyone who swears by the gold of the temple is bound by that oath.'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You blind fools! Which is greater: the gold, or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You also say, 'If anyone swears by the altar, it means nothing; but anyone who swears by the gift on the altar is bound by that oath.'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You blind men! Which is greater: the gift, or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore, anyone who swears by the altar swears by it and by everything on it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And anyone who swears by the temple swears by it and by the one who dwells in it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And anyone who swears by heaven swears by God's throne and by the one who sits on it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, teachers of the law and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You give a tenth of your spices—mint, dill and cumin. But you have neglected the more important matters of the law—justice, mercy and faithfulness. You should have practiced the latter, without neglecting the former.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You blind guides! You strain out a gnat but swallow a camel.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, teachers of the law and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and dish, but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup and dish, and then the outside also will be clean.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, teachers of the law and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You are like whitewashed tombs, which look beautiful on the outside but on the inside are full of the bones of the dead and everything unclean.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: In the same way, on the outside you appear to people as righteous but on the inside you are full of hypocrisy and wickedness.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Woe to you, teachers of the law and Pharisees, you hypocrites! You build tombs for the prophets and decorate the graves of the righteous.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And you say, 'If we had lived in the days of our ancestors, we would not have taken part with them in shedding the blood of the prophets.'
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So you testify against yourselves that you are the descendants of those who murdered the prophets.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Go ahead, then, and complete what your ancestors started!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You snakes! You brood of vipers! How will you escape being condemned to hell?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore I am sending you prophets and sages and teachers. Some of them you will kill and crucify; others you will flog in your synagogues and pursue from town to town.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And so upon you will come all the righteous blood that has been shed on earth, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zechariah son of Berekiah, whom you murdered between the temple and the altar.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Truly I tell you, all this will come on this generation.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you who kill the prophets and stone those sent to you, how often I have longed to gather your children together, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings, and you were not willing.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Look, your house is left to you desolate.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For I tell you, you will not see me again until you say, 'Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord.'
segments.speaker: Jesus
